message:
feat(extensions): re-enable JS/TS extension runtime alongside native-rust descriptors

Reverses the earlier retirement of the QuickJS-based JS/TS extension
runtime. Extensions with legacy entrypoints (.js/.ts/.mjs/.cjs/.tsx/.mts/.cts)
are once again loaded and executed via the embedded QuickJS interpreter,
restoring backward compatibility with the full extension corpus.

Key changes:

src/extensions.rs:
- Remove validate_extension_manifest() rejection of ExtensionRuntime::Js
- Re-enable ExtensionManifestSource::load_spec() to produce ExtensionLoadSpec::Js
  from manifests with runtime: "js" instead of returning an error
- Re-enable resolve_extension_load_spec() to produce JS load specs from
  .js/.ts/.mjs/.cjs/.tsx/.mts/.cts entrypoints and from index.{ts,js} in
  extension directories, instead of rejecting them
- Add JsExtensionRuntimeHandle::start_with_interceptor_and_policy() to allow
  conformance tests to supply a custom ExtensionPolicy alongside interceptors
- Add two unit tests: resolve_extension_load_spec_detects_js_entrypoint_file
  and resolve_extension_load_spec_detects_js_runtime_manifest

src/agent.rs:
- Add start_js_extension_runtime() helper (mirrors start_native_extension_runtime)
  that boots a PiJsRuntimeConfig-based QuickJS runtime with policy and repair mode
- Refactor enable_extensions() to collect both js_specs and native_specs from
  resolved extension entries, rejecting mixed runtimes with a clear error message
  ("Mixed extension runtimes are not supported in one session yet")
- Update pre-warmed runtime handling: when a pre-warmed runtime type mismatches
  the requested type (e.g. native pre-warmed but JS extensions discovered), log
  the mismatch and create the correct runtime type instead
- Update cold-boot path to select JS or native runtime based on wants_js_runtime
- Wire js_specs loading via manager.load_js_extensions() after runtime boot
- Add test: agent_session_enable_extensions_rejects_mixed_js_and_native_entries

src/main.rs:
- Scan extension entries at startup to determine has_js_extensions vs
  has_native_extensions, rejecting mixed runtimes early with user-facing error
- Add JS extension pre-warm path: when JS extensions are detected, spawn a
  background JsExtensionRuntimeHandle::start_with_policy() task (matching the
  existing native pre-warm pattern) with resolved policy, repair mode, and
  memory limits
- Extract prewarm_policy, prewarm_repair, prewarm_memory_limit_bytes above
  the prewarm branch to share between JS and native paths
- Update log messages: "native-only" -> "native-rust" for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
